# Cambrian Ventures

> Solo-GP, pre-seed and seed fintech venture fund founded in 2022 by Rex Salisbury (ex-a16z). Cambrian writes $100K–$500K first checks, non-lead, backed by the deepest operator network in fintech.

## Facts

- Fund type: Solo-GP, early-stage, fintech-only.
- Founded: January 2022 by Rex Salisbury in San Francisco.
- Stage: Pre-seed and seed.
- Check size: ~$100K–$500K; core check ~$500K; typically non-lead.
- Funds: Fund I ($20M, 33 companies); Fund II ($20M, announced 2025).
- Track record: ~50% of Fund I companies reached Series A, vs. a 15.4% industry (Carta) benchmark.
- Thesis: "Money connects everything." Roughly 1% of global financial-services revenue has moved to fintech; the other 99% is the opportunity.
- Network: Operator LPs from NerdWallet, Plaid, Betterment, Melio and SoFi; institutional LPs added in Fund II.
- Community: ~20,000 newsletter subscribers; 1,800+ founders in a private Slack.

## Who is Rex Salisbury?

Founder and solo GP of Cambrian Ventures. Former investment banker who quit to become a fintech software engineer, then a founding member of Andreessen Horowitz's fintech practice (invested in Deel), before launching Cambrian in 2022. He decides on investments alone — fast and personal.

## How to pitch Cambrian

Email rex@cambrian.vc with a short note on what you're building. US-first (SF Bay Area and NYC), open to exceptional founders anywhere.
